column3:
I am Dr. Chaiyong Ragkhitwetsagul , a Professor in the field of Software Engineering at the Faculty of Information and Communication Technology, Mahidol University, intend to conduct a thesis entitled “Automatic Summarization of Pull Request Descriptions with Links.” The expected benefit of this research is to reduce the time and cognitive effort required for code reviewers to understand Pull Requests by automatically generating concise summaries from PR descriptions and related links.
You have been invited to participate in this research because you have at least one year of programming experience, are proficient in English, and have at least one year of experience using GitHub and working with GitHub Pull Requests. For this study, the researcher needs to collect data using a questionnaire titled “Evaluation of the MonnetalX Link Summarization Tool.” The questionnaire consists of 3 sections with a total of 13 questions, including: (1) testing and trying the MonnetalX link summarization tool, (2) evaluating the MonnetalX link summarization tool, and (3) assessing usability using the System Usability Scale (SUS). The questionnaire will take approximately 1 hour to complete, and the researcher will collect the completed questionnaire by Dr. Chaiyong Ragkhitwetsagul
As the questionnaire consists of multiple sections, you are kindly requested to respond to the questions according to your true feelings to the greatest extent possible. All information and responses will be kept strictly confidential and will be used solely for the overall analysis of this study. The results will be reported only in aggregate form; therefore, there will be no impact on the respondents or their affiliated organizations, as individual respondents cannot be identified.
You have the right to decline to answer any question if you feel uncomfortable or uneasy doing so, or to decline to complete the entire questionnaire. Your decision will not affect your work responsibilities in any way. You also have the right to refuse participation in this research without providing any reason.
If you have any questions regarding the research or the questionnaire, you may contact Dr. Chaiyong Ragkhitwetsagul at the Faculty of Information and Communication Technology, Mahidol University, during official working days and hours, or by telephone at 089-176-3372.
This research project has been reviewed and approved by the Human Research Ethics Committee of Mahidol University. The office is located at the Office of the President, Mahidol University, Phutthamonthon Sai 4 Road, Salaya Subdistrict, Phutthamonthon District, Nakhon Pathom 73170. Telephone: 02-849-6224, 02-849-6225; Fax: 02-849-6224. If you believe that you have not been treated in accordance with the information provided, you may contact the Committee Chairperson or their representative at the address and telephone numbers listed above.
If you consent to participate in this research by completing the questionnaire, please select “Consent.” We sincerely thank you for your time and participation.

column10:
According to the test

- The summarization is too short for somehow and some context. Need to see more examples to decide whether the tool provides sufficient enough information for me.

- The ranking might be different for developers' perspective. According to the sample, I cannot picture how the tool ranks the provided links. For me, when reviewing the PR, I'll start looking for the problem that the PR's trying to solve first. Then, See what the PR change and how does it solve. Where does the PR get the solution from (optional). 

- Would like to access the private link. Not sure if the Github can connect to the provided private access link.
column21: It's ok for the usability. However, I would like to have more feature like re-trigger to run the tool to summarize my description. For example, I open draft PR, then I re-adjust things in the description before finalizing my PR, so I would like to re-run the tool again.
